About

I'm Pratik -- a Data Scientist by profession, a traveler by instinct, and a reader by passion.

My work has taken me deep into machine learning, generative AI, and data-driven problem solving. I've published research, built AI applications, and experimented with ways technology can simplify complex worlds. But my heart often wanders to mountains, books, and the quiet spaces where ideas grow.

On the research side, I apply deep learning to problems in cosmology. My most recent work, CosmoMamba, is the first application of state-space models to cosmological parameter inference from 2D field maps. Before that, I studied AGN-driven turbulence at National Tsing Hua University in Taiwan with Dr. Hsiang-Yi Karen Yang, and worked on neural network acceleration of N-body simulations at the University of Hawaii.

In industry, I've spent 3+ years at CertifyOS building predictive analytics systems, NLP pipelines, and automated ETL infrastructure on millions of healthcare provider records using BigQuery, Python, and GCP.

I studied Computer Science at IIIT Nagpur, where my final-year project used deep learning to extract cosmological parameters from Cosmic Microwave Background maps.

Outside of work, I find meaning in Dostoevsky's questions, Murakami's surreal silences, and the slow life of roaming beyond routines. I write on Medium about books, ideas, travel, and the ways in which technology and philosophy intersect.

My dream is simple: to live widely, think deeply, and create things that outlast fleeting trends.
